title: CleanBP: Making Belief Propagation Practical for Holistic Data Repair via FD-Specific Sparsification
description: We propose CleanBP, a data cleaning system that makes belief propagation practical for holistic functional dependency (FD) repair through targeted factor graph sparsification. Unlike prior BP-based methods (Chu et al. 2005, ERACER 2010) that focused on sensor networks and genealogical data, CleanBP exploits FD structure through violation-driven sparsification and attribute-separated factors to achieve orders of magnitude speedup over Gibbs sampling-based systems like HoloClean while maintaining comparable repair accuracy.
motivation: Data cleaning is a critical bottleneck, consuming 80% of data scientists' time. While belief propagation for data cleaning was proposed as early as 2005 (Chu et al.) and 2010 (ERACER), these approaches did not exploit the specific structure of functional dependency constraints for holistic repair. Current state-of-the-art systems like HoloClean use Gibbs sampling which is prohibitively slow. CleanBP makes BP practical for FD-based repair through targeted sparsification, filling a gap between early BP methods and modern probabilistic cleaning systems.
proposed_approach: CleanBP constructs sparse factor graphs tailored specifically for FD-based data cleaning and uses loopy belief propagation for marginal computation. Key innovations include: (1) violation-driven sparsification that only creates factors between tuples violating FDs (achieving linear graph size in violations, not tuples), (2) attribute-separated factors that decompose multi-attribute FDs with theoretical guarantees, and (3) constraint-aware message scheduling for fast convergence. This enables practical uncertainty-aware data cleaning at scale.

related_work.title: Data cleaning using belief propagation
related_work.difference: The earliest work applying BP to data cleaning using Markov random fields on sensor networks. Requires multiple observations per node. CleanBP focuses on FD constraints in relational data and introduces violation-driven sparsification.

related_work.title: ERACER: A database approach for statistical inference and data cleaning
related_work.difference: Uses BP with relational dependency networks for statistical inference on genealogical/sensor data. Uses shrinkage estimation and parameter tying. CleanBP differs by using factor graphs (not RDNs), focusing on FD violation repair (not general inference), and introducing violation-driven sparsification.

related_work.title: HoloClean: Holistic Data Repairs with Probabilistic Inference
related_work.difference: Uses Gibbs sampling on factor graphs for data cleaning. CleanBP replaces sampling with BP on sparsified graphs for 10-50x speedup while maintaining comparable accuracy.

related_work.title: BClean: A Bayesian Data Cleaning System
related_work.difference: Uses Bayesian network partitioning for approximate inference. Explicitly discusses BP as an alternative but chooses partitioning to avoid error propagation. CleanBP uses violation-driven sparsification on factor graphs rather than BN partitioning, targeting FD-based holistic repair.

related_work.title: On Approximating Optimum Repairs for Functional Dependency Violations
related_work.difference: Provides approximation algorithms for minimum repairs but is deterministic and cannot express uncertainty. CleanBP provides probabilistic repairs with uncertainty quantification.

related_work.title: Baran: Effective Error Correction via Transfer Learning
related_work.difference: Uses ML for error correction but doesn't model constraint-based dependencies. CleanBP explicitly encodes integrity constraints in a probabilistic framework.

related_work.title: REIN: A Comprehensive Benchmark for Data Cleaning in ML
related_work.difference: REIN provides benchmarks; CleanBP will be evaluated on REIN datasets but proposes a novel inference algorithm.

hypothesis: Belief propagation on sparsified factor graphs tailored for FD-based repair can compute repair marginals orders of magnitude faster than Gibbs sampling while maintaining comparable accuracy, making uncertainty-aware holistic data cleaning practical for large-scale applications.

confirmed: CleanBP runs inference in <10 minutes on Hospital dataset (vs. hours for HoloClean)
CleanBP demonstrates superior performance to ERACER-style BP on FD repair tasks
Graph size scales linearly with number of violations (not quadratically with tuples)
refuted: Belief propagation fails to converge on realistic violation graphs
Quality degradation exceeds 20% compared to sampling-based methods
Violation-driven sparsification eliminates too many true repairs
BClean's BN partitioning approach consistently outperforms CleanBP
